name: cooper
description: All-in-one SNES game development for the OpenSNES SDK + luna emulator: WLA-DX 65816 highlighting, C support via clangd, and project tooling.

scripts.test:integration: npm run compile && npm run compile-tests && vscode-test

walkthroughs.title: Get Started with Cooper
walkthroughs.description: Build, run and debug SNES games on the OpenSNES SDK + luna — in a few clicks.

steps.title: Point Cooper at your tools
steps.description:
Your game lives in its own folder; OpenSNES and luna are installed separately — **download** the prebuilt build for your architecture (no compiler to install).

**Get them:** the OpenSNES SDK zip for your OS/arch from https://github.com/k0b3n4irb/opensnes/releases (extract it — `cc65816`/`qbe`/`wla-65816` are in its `bin/`), and **luna** from https://github.com/k0b3n4irb/luna/releases/latest . Then point Cooper at them:

[Set the OpenSNES SDK path](command:workbench.action.openSettings?%22cooper.opensnesPath%22)
[Set the luna path](command:workbench.action.openSettings?%22cooper.lunaPath%22)

`cooper.lunaPath` accepts the luna binary **or** the folder that contains it.

steps.title: Open the Cooper panel
steps.description:
Click the **Cooper** icon (a gamepad) in the activity bar. Everything is one click — Build, Run, Debug, PPU viewers, and your project's functions.

[Open the Cooper panel](command:workbench.view.extension.cooper)

steps.title: Build your ROM
steps.description:
Compile with `make`. Cooper passes your SDK path automatically, so it works from any folder. Errors land in the Problems panel.

[Build (make)](command:cooper.build)

steps.title: Run a frame
steps.description:
Render a frame in luna and see it inline — your game, on cycle-accurate hardware.

[Run / Preview](command:cooper.preview)

steps.title: Debug your game
steps.description:
In the Cooper panel under **SYMBOLS**, click one of your functions to set a breakpoint, then start debugging and press **Continue**. Inspect CPU registers, memory and the live PPU at the stop.

[Start Debugging](command:cooper.debug)

media.altText: Symbols and Debug in the Cooper panel

steps.title: Inspect the PPU
steps.description:
At a debug stop, view the live **Palette**, **Sprites (OAM)** and **Tiles (VRAM)** — decoded straight from the emulator.

[Open the Dashboard](command:cooper.home)

target.description: The make target to run (default: the project's default goal, which builds the ROM).

program.description: Optional path to the .sfc/.smc ROM. If omitted, Cooper resolves it from the project's Makefile TARGET (recommended — stays correct if you restructure the project).

stopOnEntry.description: Stop at the reset vector when the ROM loads.

maxSteps.description: Instruction budget for a Continue before it pauses (no breakpoint hit).

configurationSnippets.label: Cooper: Debug SNES ROM in luna
configurationSnippets.description: Launch the project's ROM under the luna debugger (resolved from the Makefile)

cooper.opensnesPath.markdownDescription: Absolute path to the OpenSNES SDK root (the folder containing `lib/include/snes.h`). Used to generate the `.clangd` file. If empty, Cooper infers it from the project Makefile's `OPENSNES` line, then by searching parent folders.

cooper.autoConfigureClangd.markdownDescription: Automatically write a `.clangd` for C IntelliSense when you open a C file in an OpenSNES project (never overwrites an existing one). Turn off to configure manually with **Cooper: Configure clangd**.

cooper.lunaPath.markdownDescription: Path to the `luna` emulator — the **binary** or the **folder** that contains it (a user release unzips to a folder with `luna`/`luna-gui`). If empty, Cooper uses the SDK's pinned binary at `<sdk>/tools/luna-test/bin/luna`, then `luna` on your PATH.

cooper.preview.steps.markdownDescription: CPU instructions luna executes before grabbing the preview screenshot. Lower values may render a black frame before the ROM has drawn anything; 200000 is past init for typical demos.

cooper.preview.forceDisplay.markdownDescription: Pass `--force-display` so the preview bypasses INIDISP forced-blank — shows what the ROM uploaded to VRAM/CGRAM even if it left the screen blanked (e.g. a title waiting on Start).
